title: TEMPEST shielding delay — Server Room B
description: Incomplete RF shielding in Server Room B is blocking full TEMPEST recertification for Lincolnia HQ. Contractor delivery was delayed by 3 weeks due to supply chain disruption.
mitigation: Emergency vendor contract issued. Shielding materials delivered Apr 8. Installation begins Apr 11. Backup plan: request 30-day DSS extension if installation incomplete by Apr 13.

title: Classified spillage incident — uncleared network
description: A SECRET//NOFORN document was inadvertently transmitted to an unclassified email system at Texas Field Office on Apr 5. Incident Report #IR-2026-0042 filed. DSS notification completed.
mitigation: Affected systems isolated within 2 hours. DSS and ISSM notified. Sanitization completed Apr 7. Mandatory user retraining in progress. Revised data transfer SOP under review.

title: Visitor log procedures incomplete
description: DAAPM-required electronic visitor log system not fully deployed at Texas Field Office. Paper logs in use — non-compliant with current DAAPM Rev 5.
mitigation: SOP revision drafted and under leadership review. eLog system procurement approved — installation Apr 22. Personnel training scheduled same day.

title: SCIF door hardware failure — Zone 1 entrance
description: Electromagnetic lock on Zone 1 entrance at Seattle Annex failed Apr 3. Temporary mechanical lock installed as interim measure. Zone not fully TEMPEST-compliant during transition.
mitigation: ICD 705-compliant replacement hardware on order. Installation by certified locksmith scheduled Apr 15. Enhanced manual guard presence in place until resolved.

title: Annual security training overdue — 2 personnel
description: Two cleared personnel at Maryland Warehouse have not completed NISPOM-mandated annual security awareness training. System-flagged Apr 7. Accesses subject to suspension after 30 days.
mitigation: In-person training block scheduled Apr 18. Online CBT alternative authorized as backup. SSM notified both individuals directly.

title: Access roster not updated after personnel departure
description: Access control roster at Colorado Springs was not updated within the required 24-hour window following the separation of one cleared employee (Mar 28). NISPOM §10-306 compliance gap.
mitigation: Roster corrected Apr 2. Procedural review underway. Updated SOP requires dual-confirmation within 6 hours of separation. Self-inspection scheduled Apr 22.

title: Contractor clearance verification gap — 3 individuals
description: Periodic reinvestigation (PR) submissions overdue by 45 days for 3 contractor-cleared individuals at Lincolnia HQ. DoD PERSEC tracking system alert issued.
mitigation: PR packages submitted for all 3 individuals Apr 8. DISS case numbers obtained. Accesses remain active pending adjudication — legal review authorized continuation.

title: NISPOM self-inspection overdue
description: Maryland Warehouse annual NISPOM self-inspection was not completed within the required calendar year window. Last completed Jan 2025. Now 14 months overdue.
mitigation: Self-inspection checklist issued. Kickoff meeting scheduled Apr 14. Expected completion May 10. Results to be shared with DSS industrial security rep.

title: DAAPM boundary diagram out of date
description: SCIF boundary diagram for Colorado Springs was not updated to reflect minor construction changes completed Feb 12. Required DAAPM update within 30 days of physical change.
mitigation: Updated as-built drawings submitted to DSS Mar 24. Revised accreditation boundary diagram approved Mar 31.

title: Emergency destruction procedures not posted
description: Required NISPOM emergency destruction procedures placard was missing from classified storage room at California Lab. Identified during routine internal inspection Feb 18.
mitigation: Placard printed and posted Feb 19. Laminated backup copy placed in SSM binder. Added to monthly inspection checklist.

title: ICD 705 cable separation — Zone 3
description: Signal cable separation requirement in Zone 3 at California Lab was flagged during Feb audit. RED/BLACK cable runs were within minimum separation distance by 4 inches.
mitigation: Physical cable separation rerouted and verified Mar 29. ICD 705 TSCM sweep conducted Apr 1. SSM signed off Apr 2.

title: Unauthorized wireless device detected — Conference Room 4B
description: TSCM sweep at DC Metro Office on Mar 18 identified an unregistered Bluetooth-capable device in Conference Room 4B. Device traced to a personal smartwatch left by cleared employee. No data exfiltration confirmed.
mitigation: Device removed and analyzed — no hostile capability confirmed. Employee counseled. Wireless device policy brief issued to all DC Metro staff Mar 25. TSCM re-sweep completed Apr 2 — clean.
